name: Limestone Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Limestone. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for limestone cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for limestone absorption and minimal scattering

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for balanced cleaning efficiency and resolution

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage and thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal while preserving limestone surface

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for effective contaminant removal with minimal substrate damage

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning without excessive overlap

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for thorough cleaning of limestone surfaces

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning coverage

power.description: Typical range for industrial 1064 nm Nd:YAG lasers cleaning limestone; starting at 50-100 W to avoid thermal damage; max safe below 1000 W to prevent substrate cracking, based on manufacturer specs from Adapt Laser Systems and studies on stone ablation (e.g., Pou et al., Applied Surface Science, 2003).

scan_speed.description: Effective range for contamination removal; slower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy soiling like graffiti, faster (up to 5000 mm/s) for light dust; recommended 1000 mm/s for balanced efficiency on limestone, per industry standards from LaserCladding Technologies manuals and case studies on cultural heritage cleaning.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning of limestone; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces heat-affected zone (HAZ) but increases cost; 20 ns recommended for minimal thermal damage to CaCO3 substrate, from laser physics research (e.g., S. J. Henley et al., Applied Physics A, 2004).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed 1064 nm systems; 30 kHz balances cleaning speed and heat accumulation on low-conductivity limestone; higher rates (>50 kHz) risk overheating, lower for precision; sourced from academic literature on pulse train effects (e.g., J. C. S. Lai et al., Optics and Lasers in Engineering, 2010).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe removal of contaminants without ablating limestone; damage threshold ~3-5 J/cm² due to CaCO3 decomposition; 1.5 J/cm² recommended for industrial cleaning, based on ablation threshold studies (e.g., R. P. Forbes et al., Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2003).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for 1064 nm cleaning; smaller (100-300 μm) for fine details like engravings, larger (up to 1 mm) for broad surfaces; 300 μm recommended for uniform limestone cleaning, from equipment specifications by IPG Photonics and process optimization in stone conservation.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy layers; 2 passes recommended for complete removal on limestone without excessive exposure, per industry practice and case studies (e.g., European project on laser cleaning of monuments, 2015).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 50% recommended for even cleaning on irregular limestone surfaces, avoiding gaps or overexposure; range from process optimization studies (e.g., A. Nevin et al., Analytical and Bioanalytical Chemistry, 2007).
